customer_summary.summary: The UVZ web application currently runs on Angular 18 and Pattern Library 11.3.1. Security support for Angular 18 ends on 21 Nov 2025, after which no free patches are provided. To keep the application secure, compliant and maintainable, it must be upgraded now to Angular 19 together with Pattern Library 12.6.0 and all related dependencies. If the upgrade is not performed, the system will become vulnerable to unpatched security issues and may breach internal security policies, forcing the team to pay for a costly Never‑Ending Support contract.
customer_summary.workaround: Continue on Angular 18 only by purchasing Never‑Ending Support from the vendor, which incurs additional cost and does not solve the underlying obsolescence.

developer_context.big_picture: UVZ is a client‑facing web portal built with Angular (presentation layer) that consumes backend services written in Java 17. End users are internal staff and external partners who perform administrative tasks through the UI. This ticket addresses the need to move the front‑end from Angular 18 to Angular 19 and to update the Pattern Library from 11.3.1 to 12.6.0. The upgrade is required now because Angular 18 loses its free security support on 21 Nov 2025, exposing the portal to unpatched vulnerabilities. Without the upgrade the product would either become insecure or require a paid support contract, both of which are unacceptable for the business.
developer_context.scope_boundary: IN: • Upgrade Angular core, CLI and related packages to v19. • Upgrade Pattern Library (bnotk/ds‑ng) to 12.6.0. • Update Node.js, TypeScript, ng‑bootstrap (including the custom patch), ng‑select, ng‑option‑highlight, ag‑grid‑angular and ag‑grid‑community to versions compatible with Angular 19. • Verify that the vertical action bar continues to function (it is deprecated but supported until PL 13.2.0). • Identify and eliminate code, modules or styles that are exclusive to Angular 18 / PL 11.3.1 and are no longer needed. OUT: • Backend Java services, database schema, infrastructure provisioning, CI/CD pipeline scripts unrelated to the front‑end build, and any non‑UI components. • Feature work unrelated to the upgrade (e.g., new UI screens).
affected_components: Build & CI configuration for Angular (infrastructure layer)

context_boundaries.boundary: Angular 19 requires TypeScript ≥5.0 and Node.js ≥18. The current stack uses TypeScript 4.9.5 and an unspecified older Node version, so both must be upgraded before the framework can be upgraded.
source_facts: issue description: node.js listed as a required dependency

context_boundaries.boundary: The custom ng‑bootstrap patch referenced in the acceptance criteria may not be compatible with Angular 19; compatibility must be verified and the patch reapplied if needed.
source_facts: issue acceptance criteria: ng-bootstrap (Patch von UVZUSLNVV‑5824 muss berücksichtigt werden)

context_boundaries.boundary: ag‑grid‑angular and ag‑grid‑community versions used for Angular 18 may have breaking changes in their Angular 19 compatible releases; these libraries need to be upgraded and tested.
bnotk/ds‑ng version 12.6.0 is built for Angular 19; all UI components that rely on the pattern library must be compiled against the new version.
The vertical action bar is deprecated but still supported until Pattern Library 13.2.0; after the upgrade it must continue to work, but future removal should be planned.
source_facts: issue acceptance criteria: Vertical action bar is still used (deprecated but still possible to use till PL 13.2.0)

context_boundaries.boundary: Angular 18 security support ends 21 Nov 2025; staying on this version would leave the portal without critical security patches.
source_facts: issue description: support for security updates ended on 21.11.2025 for Angular 18

developer_context.architecture_notes: The UVZ system consists of five containers. The front‑end Angular application lives in the **"frontend" container** and occupies the **presentation layer** (≈287 components). It consumes services from the **application layer** (≈184 components) via HTTP/REST. The Pattern Library (bnotk/ds‑ng) is a shared UI component library also part of the presentation layer and is imported by many UI components, including the vertical action bar. The build pipeline (Webpack 5, Angular CLI) resides in the infrastructure layer of the same container. Upgrading Angular therefore touches: • The Angular core packages in the presentation layer, • The Pattern Library package, • Build tooling (Angular CLI, Webpack), • Node/TypeScript versions used by the infrastructure layer, and • All UI components that depend on the upgraded libraries. Neighboring components include the authentication guard (uses DefaultExceptionHandler), data‑grid components (ag‑grid), and UI widgets (ng‑bootstrap, ng‑select). No backend Java components are directly affected.

anticipated_questions.question: Which Node.js and TypeScript versions are required for Angular 19?
anticipated_questions.answer: Angular 19 officially supports Node.js 18 LTS (or newer) and TypeScript 5.0+. The current project uses TypeScript 4.9.5, so both Node and TypeScript must be upgraded before the Angular upgrade can succeed.

anticipated_questions.question: Are there breaking API changes in Angular 19 that will affect our existing code?
anticipated_questions.answer: Angular 19 introduces several deprecations and removal of legacy APIs (e.g., ViewEngine is fully removed, some router APIs changed). All components that use deprecated APIs will need to be refactored; the vertical action bar, being deprecated, should be checked for any removed hooks.

anticipated_questions.question: Will the custom ng‑bootstrap patch still apply after moving to Angular 19?
anticipated_questions.answer: The patch was created for Angular 18. Compatibility must be verified against the Angular 19 version of ng‑bootstrap; if the patch does not apply cleanly, it will need to be re‑implemented for the newer version.

anticipated_questions.question: Do we need to adjust the CI/CD pipeline (e.g., Angular CLI version, Docker images)?
anticipated_questions.answer: Yes. The pipeline currently uses Angular CLI 18‑lts and a Node image compatible with that version. Both the CLI and the base Node image must be updated to the versions required by Angular 19.

anticipated_questions.question: Is the vertical action bar guaranteed to keep working after the upgrade?
anticipated_questions.answer: The action bar is deprecated but still supported up to Pattern Library 13.2.0, so it should continue to work with PL 12.6.0. Nevertheless, functional tests should verify its behavior after the upgrade.
